LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FRED SUESS, MD'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND, THIRD, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND CONTINUED THE MATTER TO 04/28/11 AT 9:30AM. DEFENDANT TO GIVE NOTICE. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-25-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FRED SUESS, MD'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND, THIRD, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. A COMPLAINT CAN CONTAIN INCONSISTENT OR ALTERNATIVE THEORIES OF LIABILITY THAT ARISE OUT OF THE SAME SET OF FACTS. CROWLEY V. KATLEMAN (1994) 8 CAL 4TH 666, 690-91. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED FACTS WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO SUPPORT HER CAUSES OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FRED SUESS, M.D. DBA APPEARANCE ENHANCEMENT CLINI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER: 1) PLAINTIFF CONSENTED, 2) PLAINTIFF WAS AN APPROPRIATE CANDIDATE FOR THE PROCEDURE, 3) THE PROCEDURES WERE SUBSTANTIALLY DIFFERENT, 4) DEFENDANT MADE FALSE REPRESENTATIONS TO PLAINTIFF, 5) PLAINTIFF RELIED ON DEFENDANT'S REPRESENTATIONS, AND 6) DEFENDANT HAD A DUTY TO DISCLOSE TO PLAINTIFF HIS DECISION TO CHANGE THE PROCEDURE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-17-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-15-2012 Continued To May-21-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Pltf'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complt
Off Calendar Feb-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Claudia Lozano, Esq., of Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer, Tel. 415.397.2800, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Gary E. Moss, Esq., of Moss & Hough, Tel. 415.399.1110, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JANINE BOCCIARDI'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint is Granted. In light of the order denying defendants' first summary motion and the tentative ruling denying defendants' second summary motion, because there is no prejudice to defendants by granting this motion, the strong policy favoring permitting amendments requires the granting of the motion. In addition, the court permitted additional deposition of Miss Bocciardi. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Claudia Lozano, Esq., of Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer, Tel. 415.397.2800, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Gary E. Moss, Esq., of Moss & Hough, Tel. 415.399.1110, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: As no party objected to the tentative ruling, the court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FRED SUESS, M.D. DBA APPEARANCE ENHANCEMENT CLINIC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Denied in its entirety. The motion for summary judgment is denied because the sole issue articulated in the moving parties' separate statement expressly pertains only to the first cause action and does not address the other three claims alleged by plaintiff. The motion for summary adjudication is denied because the sole issue articulated in the moving parties' separate statement does not resolve the entirety of the first cause of action. Even surpassing these procedural deficiencies, the motion is denied because there is a triable issue whether Plaintiff gave informed consent to the surgical procedure performed by Dr. Suess. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Feb-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Claudia Lozano, Esq., of Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer, Tel. 415.397.2800, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Gary E. Moss, Esq., of Moss & Hough, Tel. 415.399.1110, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FRED SUESS, M.D. DBA APPEARANCE ENHANCEMENT CLINI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. There are triable issues whether: 1) Plaintiff consented, 2) Plaintiff was an appropriate candidate for the procedure, 3) the procedures were substantially different, 4) Defendant made false representations to Plaintiff, 5) Plaintiff relied on Defendant's representations, and 6) Defendant had a duty to disclose to Plaintiff his decision to change the procedure. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Ntc Of Pltf'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complt
Off Calendar Feb-14-2012 Continued To Feb-17-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JANINE BOCCIARDI's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint is continued on the court's own motion to February 17, 2012 to be heard after both of defendant's summary motions are heard. Granting this motion prior to the hearings on defendant's motions would effectively moot defendant's motions and thus cause prejudice to defendant. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FRED SUESS, MD'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND, THIRD, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Claudia Lozano of Lynch Giraldi & Grummer (397.2800) for defendant and moving party; Gary E. Moss of Moss & Hough (399.1110) for plaintiff and responding party. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: overruled. A complaint can contain inconsistent or alternative theories of liability that arise out of the same set of facts. Crowley v. Katleman (1994) 8 Cal 4th 666, 690-91. Further, plaintiff has pled facts with sufficient specificity to support her causes of action. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
